---
title: "CApoppy Analysis Index"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## How to use this index

This project is organized as a set of *theme-based* analyses. Each theme below lists:

- the **master** analysis file(s) to render,
- the **main questions** being answered,
- the **datasets** read by that file (as currently written in code),
- the **outputs** generated (figures/tables inside the rendered document; and any files written to disk when present).


---

## Theme A — Microgeographic variation in petal physiology & microclimate

### Master candidates
- `petal_physiology.qmd`
- `petal_microclimate_analysis.qmd`
- `petal_microclimate_analysis_2.qmd`
- `petal_ecophysiology_analysis.qmd`
- `flower_traits_fixed_block.qmd`

### Questions
- Do petal water-content / tissue-economics traits vary among nearby populations (microsites)?
- Which microclimate variables (temperature, RH, VPD, PAR/fog proxies) predict petal traits?
- Do short-term developmental microclimate windows (e.g., 4-day windows) explain trait variation?

### Data used (as referenced in code)
- `data/raw/plant/petal_weight.csv` (or `petal_weight_2025.csv` depending on file)
- `data/raw/rh_temp/*.csv` (site RH/temp logger files)
- `data/raw/Apogee_PAR/*.csv` (site PAR files)

### Outputs
- Population × block trait summaries (tables)
- Microclimate summaries, time series, and window metrics (plots/tables)
- Trait–microclimate relationships (correlations / regressions / mixed models)
- PCA-style multivariate summaries (when included)

---

## Theme B — Stomatal conductance (gsw) comparisons

### Master
- `Gsw_comparison_bypop.qmd`

### Questions
- Do gsw values differ among populations?
- Do gsw values differ by measurement surface (top vs bottom), time, or other covariates (as implemented)?

### Data used
- `data/processed/porometer_4pops.csv` (note: some scripts currently reference an absolute `~/GitHub/...` path)

### Outputs
- Comparative plots of gsw by population (and any stratifications used)
- Statistical tests / model summaries in the rendered report

---

## Theme C — Flowering timing / phenology

### Master
- `plant_flowering_analysis.qmd`

### Questions
- When do flowers open and close by population?
- How long are flowers open / fully available, and does duration differ by population?

### Data used
- `data/raw/plant/opening_camera_2025.csv`

### Outputs
- Timing/duration plots (distributions and summaries)
- Statistical comparisons (t-tests / models as implemented)

---

## Theme D — Flower shrinkage / water loss dynamics

### Master
- `Shrinkage2.qmd`

### Questions
- How do flower size/area and mass change over time (shrinkage)?
- Are shrinkage trajectories consistent among individuals/conditions?

### Data used
- `data/raw/fl_shrinkage2.csv`

### Outputs
- Shrinkage time-series style plots and summaries
- Trait relationship plots (area vs weight etc.)

---

## Theme E — Sensor QA/QC and substitution (microclimate infrastructure)

### Masters
- `sensor_comparison_analysis.qmd`
- `rh_sensor_comparison.qmd`
- `sensor_comparison_analysis.qmd` (new vs old sensors, plus matching)

### Questions
- Can new sensors substitute for old logger gaps at Pt. Conception and Cojo?
- How do RH aggregation methods (min/max/avg) affect comparability?

### Data used
- `data/raw/rh_temp/Cojo_rhtemp_2025.csv`
- `data/raw/rh_temp/Pt_Con_rhtemp_2025.csv`
- `data/raw/rh_temp/Cojo_Pt_Con_rhtemp_2025_tncsensor.csv`
- `data/raw/rh_temp/pt-conception-alternate.csv` (where applicable)

### Outputs
- Time series comparisons
- Scatterplots (old vs new)
- Bias/error metrics, regression/correlation summaries
- (Often) Bland–Altman style diagnostics

---

## Theme F — Plant / flower trait comparisons and data integration

### Masters
- `plant_trait_analysis.qmd` (traits entered directly in the file)
- `petal_trait_analysis.qmd` (merges petal weights + plant traits + porometer)
- `flower_trait_analysis.qmd` (Excel-based exploratory flower trait analysis)

### Questions
- Do plant morphological traits differ among populations at a sampling date?
- How do plant traits relate to petal traits and porometer measurements?
- What relationships exist among flower traits (length, etc.) and physiology (as included)?

### Data used
- `data/raw/plant/plant_traits.csv`
- `data/raw/plant/petal_weight.csv`
- `data/processed/porometer_4pops.csv`
- `data/raw/plant/Flower_traits.xlsx`
- Porometer Excel exports under `data/raw/porometer/...` (as referenced)

### Outputs
- Comparative plots and statistical tests
- Merged dataset summaries for downstream modeling

---

## Theme G — Theory / simulation (no field data)

### Master
- `Schoen and Ashman.qmd`

### Questions
- How does optimal floral longevity vary across parameter combinations in a Schoen & Ashman style framework?

### Data used
- None (simulation/parameter grids)

### Outputs
- Contour/isocline plots and derived summaries

---

## Theme H — Project planning / roadmap

### Master
- `research_plan.qmd`

### Purpose
- Organizes hypotheses, required datasets, and a staged analysis workflow.

### Data referenced
- `data/processed/Combined_3pops_march.csv`
- `data/raw/plant/plant_traits.csv`
- `data/processed/porometer_4pops.csv`
- `data/raw/plant/petal_weight.csv`
- `data/raw/fl_shrinkage2.csv`

### Outputs
- If the code chunks are enabled and objects exist, it includes `ggsave()` targets such as:
  - `figures/Fig1_environment_WUE.pdf`
  - `figures/Fig2_trait_syndrome.pdf`
  - `figures/Fig3_mixed_model.pdf`

---

## Theme I — Older time-series prototypes (CR6 / ovary temperature)

### Masters
- `Time_series.Rmd`
- `Time_series_CR6_fltime.Rmd`
- `Temp_time_series_CR6_ovary.Rmd`

### Questions
- What do the air/flower/ovary temperature time series look like across nights/days?

### Data used
- CR6 logger CSVs (currently referenced with absolute paths in some files)

### Outputs
- Rendered time series plots in HTML

---

## Utility / QA

### Master
- `review_dataset.qmd`

### Purpose
- Dataset review (variable counts/categories, structure checks) for petal weight data.

### Data used
- `data/raw/plant/petal_weight.csv`

### Outputs
- Rendered summaries in HTML
